text: As I stand here, I am enveloped by the bright sterility of fluorescent light and transparent barriers. The glass panels reflect my image endlessly, making me aware of my presence in a world that values observation and display. Moving toward the vibrant R+D wall, the words and screens prompt deeper reflection. The exhibition speaks of innovation, yet I sense an undercurrent of being scrutinized—not just by cameras or people, but by the very arrangement of objects and space. I am both observer and observed, and I feel a curious tension between visibility and self-protection. I am drawn to contemplate what it means to be seen, and to hide even as I participate.
